mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress forward earnings multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are significantly positive when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in 70% of months where UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in over 65% of quarters where both GDPC1 and RSAFS show positive quarter-over-quarter growth.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during an economic expansion phase reflects strong manufacturing output and capacity utilization, generating positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in 60% of observations during mid-cycle expansion periods when INDPRO increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase above 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward market returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in 75% of months following CPIAUCSL readings that exceed the 0.4% monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates robust economic activity and wage growth, which supports corporate earnings and yields positive equity returns over three months.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in 60% of months where PAYEMS increases by more than 100,000, or Rank IC falls below -0.02 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with increasing retail sales demonstrate synchronized strength in durable goods and consumer spending, driving positive short-term equity performance.
failure_condition: The combined HOUST and RSAFS signal yields negative 20-day returns in 70% of months where both series register positive month-over-month changes.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising unemployment during a late-cycle phase signals impending recessionary pressures, causing immediate negative equity price adjustments.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in 65% of observations during late-cycle periods when INDPRO falls and UNRATE rises simultaneously.
